title: Bertiera viburnoides
taxonomy: Bertiera viburnoides is classified under the family Rubiaceae and the order Gentianales. It belongs to the family of flowering plants known as angiosperms and is a member of the clade known as core eudicots. The genus Bertiera is part of the Rubiaceae family, which is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The classification of Bertiera viburnoides is further divided into its kingdom, phylum, class, and order. This complex classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Bertiera viburnoides, also known as the 'African Bertiera', is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can aid in treating fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Bertiera viburnoides include iridoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Bertiera viburnoides is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
